title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what people might value about reading and libraries and how reading habits might differ across generations.

images.label: A
images.description: A child sitting cross-legged on a colourful bean bag in a school library, completely absorbed in a large illustrated storybook

images.label: B
images.description: A university student surrounded by towering stacks of academic books in a grand library, taking handwritten notes with intense concentration

followUp.question: Do you think physical libraries are still relevant in the digital age? Why or why not?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what the advantages and disadvantages of digital reading might be compared to physical books.

images.label: D
images.description: A commuter on a busy train reading from a sleek e-reader, surrounded by other passengers who are scrolling on their phones

images.label: E
images.description: A person in bed reading from a tablet in a dimly lit room, the screen casting a faint blue glow on their face

followUp.question: Do you prefer reading physical books or digital ones, and what do you think each format offers that the other cannot?
